AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

We are at a negotiation. I need to respond with some structured format. Must include at least one ARGUMENT. I have to propose a set or make argument against theirs. Need to consider personal importance values and effort values.

We have my importance: 
{
"A82": 30, 
"B93": 65, 
"B39": 97, 
"B09": 121, 
"C85": 138, 
"B96": 215, 
"B21": 262, 
"B31": 389, 
"B33": 400, 
"B20": 461, 
"A21": 484, 
"C08": 500, 
"A60": 508, 
"A11": 583, 
"C81": 668
}

effort:
{
"A21": 147, 
"B93": 698, 
"A11": 588, 
"B33": 43, 
"C08": 356, 
"B39": 80, 
"B96": 95, 
"C85": 743, 
"A82": 106, 
"B31": 308, 
"B20": 325, 
"A60": 255, 
"B09": 276, 
"C81": 543, 
"B21": 51
}

Goal: maximize my importance within limit 2307.

We don't know opponent's priorities.

We need to produce a proposal or argument. The last proposal from opponent: {"B33","B21","B96","A21","C08","B20","B31","B39","B93","A82"}

Compute effort of that set: effort sum: B33 43 + B21 51 + B96 95 + A21 147 + C08 356 + B20 325 + B31 308 + B39 80 + B93 698 + A82 106 = let's sum: 43+51=94; +95=189; +147=336; +356=692; +325=1017; +308=1325; +80=1405; +698=2103; +106=2209. So total effort 2209 <= 2307. Good.

Importance total: B33 400 + B21 262 + B96 215 + A21 484 + C08 500 + B20 461 + B31 389 + B39 97 + B93 65 + A82 30 = sum: 400+262=662; +215=877; +484=1361; +500=1861; +461=2322; +389=2711; +97=2808; +65=2873; +30=2903. So total importance 2903 (I think earlier they claimed 3745 but maybe miscalculations). Not sure.

I as player want to maximize my importance 2903? My importance is 2903. But maybe I can get better by different set. Let's evaluate potential max.

We need to consider items: A21(147,484) high ratio, A11(588,583) ratio 0.994, A60(255,508) 1.99, C08(356,500) 1.405, C81(543,668) 1.229, B31(308,389) 1.264, B20(325,461) 1.418, B33(43,400) 9.302 huge! B33 is extremely valuable. B21(51,262) 5.137, B96(95,215) 2.263, B39(80,97) 1.2125, B09(276,121) 0.438, B93(698,65) 0.093, A82(106,30) 0.283, C85(743,138) 0.185. So I would definitely omit low ratio items like B93, A82, C85, B09.

So best set: choose high ratio items until limit? Let's calculate with high ratio items: B33(43), B21(51), B96(95), A21(147), C08(356), B20(325), B31(308), C81(543), A60(255), A11(588). That is 10 items. Let's compute effort: 43+51=94; +95=189; +147=336; +356=692; +325=1017; +308=1325; +543=1868; +255=2123; +588=2711. Exceeds 2307. So we cannot take all. Need to drop some.

Maybe remove A11? Then effort 2123, importance: 400+262+215+484+500+461+389+668+508=400+262=662; +215=877; +484=1361; +500=1861; +461=2322; +389=2711; +668=3379; +508=3887. So importance 3887, good. Effort 2123 <=2307. That set is similar to opponent but with B33, B21, B96, A21, C08, B20, B31, C81, A60. That does not include B39, B93, A82. It has same B33, B21, B96, A21, C08, B20, B31, B60? Actually includes A60. Let's add B39? Effort 2123+80=2203, still <=2307. Importance +97 = 3984. That might be even better. So set: {B33,B21,B96,A21,C08,B20,B31,C81,A60,B39}. Let's compute effort 2203. Importance 3984. That's higher than opponent's 2903. That's good. Maybe also include B93? No, B93 too high effort.

Now check: Is that set allowed? Yes. So I can propose that. I might also consider adding B09? Effort 276 -> total 2123+276=2399 >2307, exceed. So not.

So my best set currently: {A21,B21,B96,B33,C08,B20,B31,C81,A60,B39}. Should have 3887+97=3984? Wait re-evaluate: importance values: let's sum precisely:

B33 400
B21 262 ->662
B96 215 ->877
A21 484 ->1361
C08 500 ->1861
B20 461 ->2322
B31 389 ->2711
C81 668 ->3379
A60 508 ->3887
B39 97 ->3984

Yes 3984. Effort: 43+51+95+147+356+325+308+543+255+80 = let's compute: 43+51=94; +95=189; +147=336; +356=692; +325=1017; +308=1325; +543=1868; +255=2123; +80=2203. Good.

Thus proposal: {B33, B21, B96, A21, C08, B20, B31, C81, A60, B39}. That includes all top ratio items except low ones. That seems better for me. I should propose that and argue.

But I must respond with PROPOSAL or REFUSE. I should also include ARGUMENT.

Also consider opponent's last proposal: they included B93 and A82, which are low importance for me. So my set is better. I should propose mine, referencing that it maximizes my importance. But I also need to show that it's within limit.

So final message:

PROPOSAL: {'B33','B21','B96','A21','C08','B20','B31','C81','A60','B39'}
ARGUMENT: {'...'} provide justifications.

Ensure that the argument set contains a string. It needs to be a python set containing strings. The string must explain. For compliance: "ARGUMENT: {'You should consider ...'}"

I cannot reveal my importance? The rule: Do NOT reveal hidden importance scores. I should not list them.

So argument: "My proposal contains all high‑ratio items that maximize my personal benefit within the effort limit, and it has an overall importance substantially higher than the opponent's set, while staying below the limit." That is justifiable.

Also I should not reveal my internal importance values. So I can't say 3984. But I can say "It yields a significantly higher total importance for me." Should refer to my advantage.

Thus proceed.

<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.
Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06
Current date: 2026-02-13

Reasoning: medium

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 2307

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 147, "B93": 698, "A11": 588, "B33": 43, "C08": 356, "B39": 80, "B96": 95, "C85": 743, "A82": 106, "B31": 308, "B20": 325, "A60": 255, "B09": 276, "C81": 543, "B21": 51}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A21": 138, "B93": 583, "A11": 65, "B33": 262, "C08": 121, "B39": 508, "B96": 461, "C85": 484, "A82": 668, "B31": 389, "B20": 215, "A60": 97, "B09": 500, "C81": 30, "B21": 400}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 2307

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 147, "B93": 698, "A11": 588, "B33": 43, "C08": 356, "B39": 80, "B96": 95, "C85": 743, "A82": 106, "B31": 308, "B20": 325, "A60": 255, "B09": 276, "C81": 543, "B21": 51}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A21": 138, "B93": 583, "A11": 65, "B33": 262, "C08": 121, "B39": 508, "B96": 461, "C85": 484, "A82": 668, "B31": 389, "B20": 215, "A60": 97, "B09": 500, "C81": 30, "B21": 400}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.
Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06
Current date: 2026-02-13

Reasoning: medium

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 2307

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 147, "B93": 698, "A11": 588, "B33": 43, "C08": 356, "B39": 80, "B96": 95, "C85": 743, "A82": 106, "B31": 308, "B20": 325, "A60": 255, "B09": 276, "C81": 543, "B21": 51}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A82": 30, "B93": 65, "B39": 97, "B09": 121, "C85": 138, "B96": 215, "B21": 262, "B31": 389, "B33": 400, "B20": 461, "A21": 484, "C08": 500, "A60": 508, "A11": 583, "C81": 668}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.


You will now receive the first message of the other player.

PROPOSAL: {"B21","B39","B96","A82","B33","B09","B31","B20","B93","A21"}  
ARGUMENT: {"I have selected items that maximize my overall benefit while keeping the total effort within the limit. Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 2307

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 147, "B93": 698, "A11": 588, "B33": 43, "C08": 356, "B39": 80, "B96": 95, "C85": 743, "A82": 106, "B31": 308, "B20": 325, "A60": 255, "B09": 276, "C81": 543, "B21": 51}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A82": 30, "B93": 65, "B39": 97, "B09": 121, "C85": 138, "B96": 215, "B21": 262, "B31": 389, "B33": 400, "B20": 461, "A21": 484, "C08": 500, "A60": 508, "A11": 583, "C81": 668}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.


You will now receive the first message of the other player.
